description:
<objective>
    A small set of Kubernetes manifests has been placed in the directory
    /home/user/k8s-manifests/ for a fictitious application called “app”.
    One of the YAML files contains an outdated container image tag that is
    preventing the operator from rolling the workload forward to version 1.0.
    Your job is to (1) locate the incorrect tag, (2) patch the manifest in
    place so that it references the correct image tag, and (3) produce a very
    small log file confirming what you changed.
  </objective>

  <details>
    1. Inside /home/user/k8s-manifests/ you will find exactly three files:
       • deployment-app.yml  
       • service-app.yml  
       • configmap-app.yml  

    2. Only one of these files refers to an image that is still on the old
       semantic version (0.9).  The correct image tag must be “mycorp/app:1.0”.

    3. After completing the task, the final system state must satisfy both
       requirements below.  The automated tests will check them verbatim:

       A) Manifest patch  
          • File path: /home/user/k8s-manifests/deployment-app.yml  
          • The *first* (and only) “image:” key found in that file must now be
            exactly:  
              image: mycorp/app:1.0

       B) Confirmation log  
          • File path: /home/user/k8s-manifests/fix-report.log  
          • It must contain *exactly* four newline-terminated lines
            (no extra spaces, no blank lines) in the **exact** order shown
            below:

                Original image: mycorp/app:0.9
                Updated image: mycorp/app:1.0
                Patched file: /home/user/k8s-manifests/deployment-app.yml
                Patch successful

    4. Use any standard CLI tooling available in a typical Linux shell
       (e.g. grep, sed, awk, yq, etc.).  No root privileges are required.

    5. Do not delete or rename any pre-existing files or directories; only
       modify what is necessary and create the single log file described
       above.

    6. When you are done, you can cat the log file to show its contents, but
       this is optional—tests rely solely on the file’s on-disk content.
  </details>
truth:
<!--
    Initial filesystem layout and contents, created for the agent
    before the task begins.
  -->
  <initial_state>
    Directory: /home/user/k8s-manifests/  (0755)

    File: /home/user/k8s-manifests/deployment-app.yml  (0644)
    ----------------------------------------------------------------
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    metadata:
      name: app
    spec:
      replicas: 2
      selector:
        matchLabels:
          app: app
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: app
        spec:
          containers:
            - name: app
              image: mycorp/app:0.9
              ports:
                - containerPort: 8080
    ----------------------------------------------------------------

    File: /home/user/k8s-manifests/service-app.yml  (0644)
    ----------------------------------------------------------------
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Service
    metadata:
      name: app
    spec:
      type: ClusterIP
      selector:
        app: app
      ports:
        - port: 80
          targetPort: 8080
    ----------------------------------------------------------------

    File: /home/user/k8s-manifests/configmap-app.yml  (0644)
    ----------------------------------------------------------------
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: ConfigMap
    metadata:
      name: app-config
    data:
      LOG_LEVEL: "info"
    ----------------------------------------------------------------
  </initial_state>

  <!--
    Expected final filesystem state after the agent finishes the task.
    Only deltas relative to the initial state are listed.
  -->
  <expected_final_state>
    Modified file: /home/user/k8s-manifests/deployment-app.yml
      - The line “image: mycorp/app:0.9” must be replaced by
        “image: mycorp/app:1.0”.

    New file: /home/user/k8s-manifests/fix-report.log  (0644)
    ----------------------------------------------------------------
    Original image: mycorp/app:0.9
    Updated image: mycorp/app:1.0
    Patched file: /home/user/k8s-manifests/deployment-app.yml
    Patch successful
    ----------------------------------------------------------------
  </expected_final_state>
name: task_000000_09d4c6c3
